items.title: EPSS Surge: CVE-2023-35924
items.analysis: EPSS exploitation probability for CVE-2023-35924 surged from 49% to 52% (percentile 99%), indicating a significant increase in predicted or observed exploitation activity for this 2023-era vulnerability. It is not (yet) in CISA KEV — this signal typically precedes KEV listing and press coverage. GLPI is a free asset and IT management software package. Starting in version 10.0.0 and prior to version 10.0.8, GLPI inventory endpoint can be used to drive a SQL injection attack. By default, GLPI inventory endpoint requires no authentication. Version 10.0.8 has a patch for this issue. As a workaround, one may disable native inventory.
items.description: GLPI is a free asset and IT management software package. Version 10.0.8 has a patch for this issue. As a workaround, one may disable native inventory.

priority.reason: EPSS 52% probability of exploitation within 30 days. CVSS v3 base score 8.6.

impact.how_to_check: Identify systems running the software class described in the report and review vendor advisories for applicability.

items.tanium_hint: No single product is named. Use Installed Applications to inventory the software class in the report, then the Open Ports sensor to surface exposed services worth prioritizing.
items.rapid7_hint: In InsightVM, find the vulnerability check matching the software in this report, confirm it's in your active scan template, and prefer a credentialed scan for reliable confirmation over a version-only match.
